displayTitle: The Inverse Square Law of Illumination

beats.narration: Here's a quiet thief on every inspection: distance. Pull your work light back, and the lux on the surface falls fast — here's how fast.

payload.title: INVERSE SQUARE
payload.sub: How distance quietly steals the light off your surface.

beats.narration: Illuminance is intensity over distance squared. Double the distance, and the same light spreads over four times the area — one quarter the lux.

payload.heading: Light falls with distance squared
payload.note: E = I/D² · DOUBLE DISTANCE → ONE QUARTER LUX

beats.narration: Watch the meter: 1,000 lux at one meter, just 250 at two, and 111 at three — maybe below your procedure minimum.
Before buying a brighter lamp, move the one you have. Distance is a lighting decision — every doubling costs you three quarters of your lux.

payload.sub: Every doubling costs you three quarters of your lux.
